mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in real activity proxied by rising retail sales signals durable household demand and near-term earnings support, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when RSAFS continues to trend upward.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest available value was 509025 as of one month before 2018-09-15
failure_condition: Reject if months with positive 3-month RSAFS growth do not have higher average 20-day S&P 500 returns than months with negative 3-month RSAFS growth over a walk-forward test, or if the return spread is negative.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in FEDFUNDS tightens discount rates and raises financing costs, reducing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days after the policy-rate trend accelerates.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value was 1.91 as of one month before 2018-09-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered tightening episodes produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward instances or if average triggered returns exceed unconditional average returns.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is already below 4.0% and CPI is rising, labor-market tightness increases inflation pressure and expected Fed restraint, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 4.0 and CPIAUCSL rises over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest available value was 3.9 as of one month before 2018-09-15
regime_hint: late-cycle tight labor market with rising inflation
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition UNRATE < 4.0 and positive 3-month CPIAUCSL growth is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rising INDPRO trend signals improving production volumes and operating leverage for cyclical firms, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when industrial output momentum is positive.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest available value was 108.2317 as of one month before 2018-09-15
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO changes are not associated with higher 20-day S&P 500 returns than negative 3-month INDPRO changes in a rolling walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts signals weakening interest-rate-sensitive demand and future construction-linked earnings pressure, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls > 10% year over year while FEDFUNDS is rising over 6 months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest available value was 1168 as of two months before 2018-09-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered housing-slowdown episodes have nonnegative average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if their downside hit rate is not above the unconditional downside hit rate.

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized strength in GDPC1 and RSAFS signals broad demand growth that supports sales and earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 year-over-year growth > 2% and RSAFS 3-month change > 0
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 latest available value was 18514.595 as of five months before 2018-09-15
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDPC1 and RSAFS growth condition fails to deliver higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than periods when either condition is absent.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating CPI together with rising FEDFUNDS signals that inflation is forcing tighter monetary policy, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth > 3% and FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest available value was 251.846 as of one month before 2018-09-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered inflation-tightening observations produce positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or if average triggered returns are not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate combined with slowing payroll growth signals deterioration in household income and recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over 3 months and PAYEMS 3-month growth decelerates
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest available value was 149279 as of one month before 2018-09-15
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor-deterioration trigger is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if downside frequency is not above unconditional downside frequency.
